case_title: WAYNE TAM VS. VAN NESS AUTO BODY et al

docket_entries.description: MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-20-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS VAN NESS AUTO BODY, TONY FENG AND STANLEY ZHU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE POLICE REPORT IS PRIVILEGED AND MAY NOT BE CONSIDERED IN DETERMINING WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED COGNIZABLE CLAIMS, THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS TO SUPPORT ALL THREE CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT HUIZHEN YUAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT HUIZHEN ("SAMMI") YUAN'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS) AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. SEE CCP 430.10(E). THE CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO DEFENDANT YUAN ARE SOLELY BASED ON THE ALLEGATION THAT SHE IS LIABLE FOR CALLING THE POLICE ON PLAINTIFF. THIS CONDUCT IS ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER HAGBERG V. CALIFORNIA FEDERAL BANK FSB (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 350. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Continued To Sep-25-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HUIZHEN YUAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Huizhen ("Sammi") Yuan's demurrer to the first cause of action (intentional infliction of emotional distress) and third cause of action (negligence) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action. See CCP 430.10(e). The causes of action as to Defendant Yuan are solely based on the allegation that she is liable for calling the police on Plaintiff. This conduct is absolutely privileged under Hagberg v. California Federal Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Continued To Apr-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The OSC is reset to Apr-30-2013 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Off Calendar Jan-04-2013 Continued To Mar-12-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-25-2012 Continued To Jan-08-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Off Calendar Sep-20-2012 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-13-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Continued To Oct-03-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2012 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony P. X. Bothwell, Esq. of the Law Office of Anthony Bothwell, Tel. 415.370.9571, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS VAN NESS AUTO BODY, TONY FENG and STANLEY ZHU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that the police report is privileged and may not be considered in determining whether plaintiff has alleged cognizable claims, the complaint alleges sufficient ultimate facts to support all three claims. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony P. X. Bothwell, Esq. of the Law Office of Anthony Bothwell, Tel. 415.370.9571, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS VAN NESS AUTO BODY, TONY FENG and STANLEY ZHU'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
